languageModelTools.name: dapper_cli
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run one Dapper debugger command in a pdb-like style.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run Dapper debugger commands in a pdb-like style. Start with `help` to see supported commands plus the underlying Dapper tools and their arguments. Commands can be chained with `;` and run sequentially.

command.description: One or more pdb-like debugger commands. The current CLI supports help/h, run, launch, sessions, info, inspect, state, diff, pause, restart, continue/c, next/n, step/s, finish, quit/q, break/b, breaks, clear, disable, enable, print/p, where/bt, locals, globals, list/l, up, down, and frame. `break` accepts file paths, unique Python filename stems like `main:65`, current-stack function names like `handler:42`, plus `if` conditions and `log` messages. `breaks` lists all breakpoints or filters to one file or line. Separate sequential commands with ';'.

sessionId.description: Debug session ID. Required when more than one Dapper session is active.

threadId.description: Thread to target for execution or inspection commands. Omit to reuse the selected thread when possible.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_launch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Launch Python code under a new Dapper debug session and warn about stale tracked sessions.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Start a Dapper Python debug session for a file, module, or named configuration. Prefers the project interpreter when possible, can wait for the first stop, and warns when other tracked sessions are already active, especially on the same program or module.

sessionName.description: Optional display name for the new Python debug session.

currentFile.description: Launch the active Python file. Use this when a Python editor is already active.

file.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path to a Python file to debug.

module.description: Python module name to run, like `python -m package.module`.

configName.description: Name of an existing Dapper launch configuration from launch.json or saved Dapper settings.

target.description: Choose exactly one launch target: currentFile, file, module, or configName. Omit to use the active Python file.

args.description: Command-line arguments passed to the Python program or module.

cwd.description: Working directory for the Python debug session.

env.description: Extra environment variables for the Python debuggee.

moduleSearchPaths.description: Additional Python import search paths prepended for module resolution.

venvPath.description: Path to a virtual environment whose Python interpreter should launch the debuggee when possible.

pythonPath.description: Absolute path to the Python interpreter that should launch the debuggee when possible.

stopOnEntry.description: Whether the Python debuggee should stop immediately on entry (default: true).

justMyCode.description: Whether to focus Python debugging on user code (default: true).

subprocessAutoAttach.description: Automatically attach to supported Python child processes started by the debuggee.

waitForStop.description: If true, wait for the new Python debug session to stop before returning and include an initial snapshot when available.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get either a snapshot of the current Python debug state or a diff since a checkpoint.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read Python debugger state in one of two modes: `snapshot` for the current stop reason, call stack, locals, globals, and thread state; or `diff` for changes since a checkpoint. Use this as the main read-only state tool for paused Python code.

mode.description: Read mode. Use `snapshot` for current state or `diff` for changes since a checkpoint.

sessionId.description: Debug session ID. Omit to use the active session.

threadId.description: Thread to inspect or refresh before reading state. Omit to use the first stopped thread.

depth.description: Max stack frames to include when `mode` is `snapshot` (default: 5).

sinceCheckpoint.description: Checkpoint number to diff from when `mode` is `diff`.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_execution
languageModelTools.userDescription: Control Python debug execution, including terminate for session cleanup.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a Python debug action such as next, stepIn, stepOut, continue, pause, restart, or terminate. Use terminate for cleanup; use `report: true` with next, stepIn, stepOut, or continue to wait for the next stop and return a snapshot plus diff.

sessionId.description: Debug session ID. Omit to use the active session.

threadId.description: Thread to target for thread-scoped actions. Omit to use the first stopped thread when applicable.

report.description: If true for next/stepIn/stepOut/continue, wait for the next stop and return a snapshot plus diff.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_evaluate
languageModelTools.userDescription: Evaluate Python expressions in the current Python debug frame.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Evaluate one or more Python expressions in the context of the current Python debug frame. Returns the result, type, and any errors. WARNING: expressions execute in the Python debuggee process. You may pass a single expression as a string via 'expression', or multiple via 'expressions' array.

sessionId.description: Debug session ID. Omit to use the active session.

expression.description: A single Python expression to evaluate.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_breakpoints
languageModelTools.userDescription: List or manage breakpoints for Python debugging, including adapter verification when available.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List Python debug breakpoints or add, remove, clear, disable, or enable them. `action=list` returns the current VS Code breakpoint set and merges adapter verification into `verified` when a Dapper Python debug session is active.

sessionId.description: Debug session ID. Omit to use the active Dapper session when available.

action.description: Use `list` to inspect breakpoints or `add`/`remove`/`clear`/`disable`/`enable` to modify them.

file.description: Absolute or workspace-relative file path. Required for add/remove/clear, optional as a filter for list.

lines.description: Line numbers for add/remove actions, or an optional line filter when action=list.

logMessage.description: Log message template for add. Use {expr} for interpolation.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_variable
languageModelTools.userDescription: Inspect a Python variable or expression result, expanding children recursively.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspect a Python variable or expression result from the active debug session, expanding children recursively up to a specified depth.

sessionId.description: Debug session ID. Omit to use the active session.

expression.description: Python expression to evaluate and inspect.

depth.description: Max recursion depth for child expansion (default: 2, max: 5).

languageModelTools.name: dapper_session_info
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get metadata, readiness, and breakpoint lifecycle details for active Dapper Python debug sessions.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get metadata about the active Dapper Python debug session or sessions, including debugger readiness, breakpoint registration completion, accepted or pending or rejected breakpoints, the last transition, and the last debugger-side error.

sessionId.description: Debug session ID. Omit to list all active Dapper sessions.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_autofix
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run Ruff safe autofix for the current workspace or selected files.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run Ruff safe autofix for the current workspace or selected files. Applies changes by default and supports preview mode via apply=false.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to choose the workspace root and search upward for Python environments and Ty/Ruff config files.

files.description: Optional file paths to pass through to Ruff autofix.

apply.description: Whether to apply fixes. Defaults to true; set false for preview diff mode.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_environment
languageModelTools.userDescription: Report the selected Python environment plus Ty and Ruff availability for the current workspace.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Report the selected Python interpreter, search roots, Ty availability, Ruff availability, and Ty/Ruff config discovery for the current workspace or a specified search root.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to choose the workspace root and search upward for Python environments and Ty/Ruff config files.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_diagnostics
languageModelTools.userDescription: Report Python diagnostics for the current workspace using the currently integrated backends.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Report structured Python diagnostics for the current workspace or selected files. Returns normalized diagnostics from Ruff and Ty with grouped summary statistics (countsByFile, countsByCode, countsBySeverity, hotspots) so you can quickly see where problems cluster. Use pathFilter to focus on source or test code. The summary is computed on all matching diagnostics before the limit is applied, so you always get the full picture even when limiting the detail list.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to choose the workspace root and search upward for Python environments and Ty/Ruff config files.

files.description: Optional file paths to pass through to the diagnostics backend.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of normalized diagnostics to return in the detail list. Summary statistics always reflect all matching diagnostics.

pathFilter.description: Filter diagnostics by path class. 'source' excludes test files, 'tests' returns only test files, 'all' (default) returns everything.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_format
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run Ruff formatting for the current workspace or selected files.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run Ruff formatting for the current workspace or selected files. Applies formatting by default and supports preview diff mode via apply=false.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to choose the workspace root and search upward for Python environments and Ty/Ruff config files.

files.description: Optional file paths to pass through to Ruff format.

apply.description: Whether to apply formatting. Defaults to true; set false for preview diff mode.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_imports
languageModelTools.userDescription: Clean up unused imports, sort/organize imports, or both.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Clean up unused imports (F401), sort/organize imports (isort-style), or both in a single call. Set mode to 'cleanup' for unused import removal, 'organize' for import sorting, or 'all' (default) to run both. Supports preview diff mode via apply=false.

mode.description: Which import operation to run. 'cleanup' removes unused imports (F401), 'organize' sorts imports (isort-style), 'all' (default) runs both.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to choose the workspace root and search upward for Python environments and Ruff config files.

files.description: Optional file paths to scope the import operation to.

apply.description: Whether to apply changes. Defaults to true; set false for preview diff mode.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_project_model
languageModelTools.userDescription: Report Python source roots, test roots, config files, and package boundaries for the current workspace.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Report a structured Python project model for the current workspace or search root. The result includes search roots, source roots, test roots, detected config files, package boundaries, and the selected Python interpreter summary.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to choose the workspace root and anchor project-model discovery to a nested project root.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_typecheck
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run Ty-backed Python type checking for the current workspace or selected files.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run Ty-backed Python type checking for the current workspace or selected files. Returns normalized Ty diagnostics with grouped summary statistics (countsByFile, countsByCode, countsBySeverity, hotspots) so you can quickly see where type errors cluster. Use pathFilter to focus on source or test code. The summary is computed on all matching diagnostics before the limit is applied.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to choose the workspace root and search upward for Python environments and Ty/Ruff config files.

files.description: Optional file paths to pass through to Ty.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of normalized Ty diagnostics to return in the detail list. Summary statistics always reflect all matching diagnostics.

pathFilter.description: Filter diagnostics by path class. 'source' excludes test files, 'tests' returns only test files, 'all' (default) returns everything.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_symbol
languageModelTools.userDescription: Resolve Python definitions, references, implementations, or hover information through the active semantic backend.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Resolve Python definitions, references, implementations, or hover information for a symbol at a given file position. Uses VS Code language feature providers, preferring Ty-backed semantics when Ty is available and falling back to the active Python semantic provider otherwise.

action.description: The symbol query to run at the given file position.

file.description: Absolute or workspace-relative file path containing the symbol occurrence.

line.description: 1-based line number for the symbol occurrence.

column.description: 1-based column number for the symbol occurrence. Defaults to 1.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to resolve relative file paths and choose the workspace root.

languageModelTools.name: dapper_python_rename
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run semantic rename for a Python symbol occurrence and optionally apply the resulting workspace edits.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run semantic rename for a Python symbol occurrence using the active semantic backend. Returns a normalized summary of the resulting workspace edits and applies them by default unless apply=false is requested.

file.description: Absolute or workspace-relative file path containing the symbol occurrence to rename.

line.description: 1-based line number for the symbol occurrence.

column.description: 1-based column number for the symbol occurrence. Defaults to 1.

apply.description: Whether to apply the rename edit to the workspace. Defaults to true; set false for preview mode.

searchRootPath.description: Optional absolute path used to resolve relative file paths and choose the workspace root.

dapper.logLevel.description: Sets the verbosity of logging for the Dapper Debugger.

dapper.logToConsole.description: When enabled, logs will also be written to the VS Code developer tools console.

dapper.python.installMode.description: Strategy for making the dapper Python package available. 'auto' uses the workspace venv's interpreter and injects dapper via PYTHONPATH (never modifies the workspace venv); falls back to a managed venv with PyPI if no bundled wheels are available. 'wheel'/'pypi' install into a managed venv. 'workspace' skips venv and uses the workspace interpreter as-is.

dapper.python.baseInterpreter.description: Absolute path to the base Python interpreter used to create the managed venv. If empty, the extension will use 'python' (Windows) or 'python3' (POSIX) from PATH.

dapper.python.forceReinstall.description: Force reinstall of the dapper package into the managed environment on activation.

dapper.python.expectedVersion.description: Override the expected dapper package version. By default the extension's version is used.

dapper.debugger.logFile.description: Path to a persistent log file for the Python debugger process. When set, all log output (including DEBUG lines with full message contents) is written here instead of a temporary file that is discarded after the session. Supports the ${workspaceFolder} variable.

enumDescriptions: Log everything, including the full contents of every DAP message.
dapper.debugger.logLevel.description: Log level for the Python debugger process log file. Set to TRACE to capture the full contents of every DAP message.

dapper.hotReload.autoOnSave.description: Automatically send dapper/hotReload when a saved Python file belongs to the active stopped Dapper debug session.

dapper.agent.executionHistorySize.description: Maximum number of execution-history journal entries to retain per active Dapper debug session. Older entries are dropped from the in-memory ring buffer.

module.description: Python module name to run (equivalent to `python -m <module>`).

moduleSearchPaths.description: Additional directories prepended to module resolution (PYTHONPATH-like).

venvPath.description: Path to a virtual environment used to select the Python interpreter for launch.

args.description: Command line arguments passed to the program.

subprocessAutoAttach.description: Automatically attach to supported Python child processes started by the debuggee.

cwd.description: The working directory for the debug session.

debugServer.description: Port number for the debug server.

useIpc.description: Use IPC for communication between debug adapter and launcher.

frameEval.description: Enable frame evaluation for better performance.

inProcess.description: Run the debug adapter in the same process as the extension.

stopOnEntry.description: Break immediately when the program starts.

host.description: Host address to connect to for remote debugging

port.description: Port number to connect to for remote debugging

processId.description: Process ID to attach to, or '${command:pickProcess}' to pick

useIpc.description: Use IPC for communication with the debugger backend

frameEval.description: Enable frame evaluation for faster breakpoints

justMyCode.description: Only debug user code (skip library code)

stopOnEntry.description: Stop on the first line of the program
